pattern: With only three possible item values, the best sum for a fixed count always takes every +1 it can first, then free zeros, then the fewest possible -1s.
hints: There are only three kinds of items: +1, 0, and -1 -- to maximize the sum you always prefer taking a +1 over a 0, and a 0 over a -1.
Take as many of the +1 items as you can (up to k of them), then fill remaining picks with 0 items since they don't hurt the sum.
Only once both of those run out should any -1 items be forced into the remaining picks; the final sum is just (ones taken) minus (negative-ones taken).

edge_inputs.description: k smaller than the ones available
k equals the total number of items, every item must be taken
no ones available at all, best you can do is take zeros before any negative ones
no zeros available, forces straight from ones into negative ones

stress.description: random small counts across the whole bounded domain
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    numOnes = rng.randint(0, 50)
    numZeros = rng.randint(0, 50)
    numNegOnes = rng.randint(0, 50)
    k = rng.randint(0, numOnes + numZeros + numNegOnes)
    return [numOnes, numZeros, numNegOnes, k]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def kItemsWithMaximumSum(self, numOnes, numZeros, numNegOnes, k):
        ones = min(numOnes, k)
        k -= ones
        zeros = min(numZeros, k)
        k -= zeros
        negs = min(numNegOnes, k)
        return ones - negs
